docs.title: Research data management
docs.text: Documentation about data management with QBiC services.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: Personal access token
docs.text:
Before you can begin with downloading any data via HTTPS, you need to tell the download server who you are. We enforce a token-based authentication (personal access token: PAT) so you are not required to expose you password with anyone or with any system.

Tip

You can create as many tokens as you like, however consider them as a secret.

docs.title: Generate a token
docs.text:
First of all, navigate to your PAT overview page in your profile overview (top-right corner)

You should now be able to see the PAT token overview page:

Personal access tokens have a life-time, which can be set based on your requirements. Your token expires automatically, there is nothing you have to do manually.

Token visibility

A generated token will be only visible in its raw notation once! Make sure to store it safely in your local password manager, you will not be able to access the token value again.

docs.title: Manage tokens
docs.text:
You can see your created tokens in the overview, however since they are instantly encrypted after generation, you cannot access their raw value anymore. We encourage you to use meaningful descriptions for your tokens, so you remember for what purpose you have created them.

Warning

If you are unsure that your PAT got exposed or shared with untrusted parties, delete them right away.

docs.title: Download measurement data
docs.text: This section gives an overview of how to download big chunks of measurement data from QBiC's data manager.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Access to the project of interest
  • A client software that supports HTTP (e.g. cURL or GNU Wget)
  • A connection to the internet

docs.title: Process
docs.text:
1. Create a personal access token (PAT)
  2. Acquire download URL(s)
  3. Start the download and get a coffee

docs.title: Download datasets
docs.text:
We show how to run the download via the two popular command line clients cURL and GNU Wget.

Of course, you can use any other software that supports HTTP.

The commands contain two placeholders, where you have to provide your custom input:

  • ACCESS_TOKEN: your generated personal access token
  • MEASUREMENT_ID: the id of the measurement of interest to download
curlwget 
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <ACCESS_TOKEN>" -O https://rdm.qbic/download/measurements/<MEASUREMENT_ID> 

wget -H "Authorization: Bearer <ACCESS_TOKEN>" https://rdm.qbic/download/measurements/measurements/<MEASUREMENT_ID>
